skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Nano Banana 2 (Gemini-family flash-tier text-to-image) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents Nano Banana 2's strengths (rapid iteration, in-image typography rendering, predictable framing, optional web-grounded context), the resolution-tier pricing, the safety-tolerance dial, and when to route to Nano Banana Pro / GPT Image 2 / Flux 2 / Seedream instead. Calls `runcomfy run google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "nano banana", "nano-banana-2", "nano banana 2", "google image gen", "gemini image", or any explicit ask to generate with this model.
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Nano Banana 2 via RunComfy CLI with model-optimized prompting patterns.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: This skill is a markdown documentation file (SKILL.md, 207 lines) with no executable code. All 88 static findings are false positives: the 'external_commands' detections are backtick-delimited bash examples showing how to invoke the `runcomfy run google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image` CLI command, not actual code execution. The 'network' findings are hardcoded URLs to the legitimate runcomfy.com and runcomfy.net domains. The 'filesystem' references document the standard token storage location (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). The file contains no obfuscation, no prompt injection, no credential harvesting, and no data exfiltration patterns. The Security & Privacy section explicitly documents the security model (token storage with 0600 permissions, no shell expansion of prompts, outbound endpoint whitelist).

These are documentation examples, not executable code. The skill is a markdown prompt/skill file that provides instructions to the AI agent, which then executes commands via the user's own CLI. No injection risk detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a pure markdown documentation skill with no executable code. The 'backtick execution' detections are false positives triggered by markdown code-block syntax used for documentation purposes only.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to legitimate service domains
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com and runcomfy.net — the legitimate service that hosts the Nano Banana 2 model API. These are documentation links for the homepage, model page, and troubleshooting docs. No suspicious or unknown domains detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to the documented runcomfy.com and runcomfy.net domains. The Security & Privacy section confirms these are the only outbound endpoints used.

low_findings.title: Documentation references to token storage path
low_findings.description: The skill documents the standard token storage path at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This is informational documentation in the Security & Privacy section, not actual file system access code. The file notes token permissions are 0600 (owner-only).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is documentation about the CLI's token storage behavior, not code that accesses the filesystem. The skill is a markdown file with no execution capability.

content.user_title: Generate Images with Google Nano Banana 2
content.value_statement: Naive prompting against Nano Banana 2 wastes the model's strengths. This skill bundles the model's documented prompting patterns so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce sharper images on the first try — from rapid drafts to typography-perfect brand assets.
actual_capabilities: Invoke google/nano-banana-2/text-to-image via the local RunComfy CLI with correct JSON input
Apply model-optimized prompting patterns: subject-first declarative grammar, exact text quoting for typography, and style-anchor selection
Select appropriate resolution tiers (0.5K drafts through 4K finals) and aspect ratios from the 11 supported values
Enable web-grounded generation via enable_web_search for current events and real entities
Route to sibling models (Nano Banana Pro, GPT Image 2, Flux 2, Seedream) when the use case demands it
Produce batch variants (up to 4 images per request) for ideation rounds with consistent seed locking
limitations: Still images only — no video output on this endpoint
Maximum 4 images per request — not designed for bulk batch jobs
Web search adds latency and cost — should only be enabled on demand
Higher resolutions (2K, 4K) cost more — defaults to 1K unless explicitly requested
Image editing requires the separate /edit endpoint, not this text-to-image skill

use_cases.title: Marketing thumbnail ideation
use_cases.description: Generate batches of 4 social-platform-native thumbnails at 0.5K for rapid iteration, then promote the winner to 2K for final delivery.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing designers and social media managers

use_cases.title: Brand asset production with typography
use_cases.description: Create product cards, posters, and hero images with predictable in-image text rendering by quoting exact characters and specifying font style.

use_cases.title: Web-grounded current-event imagery
use_cases.description: Generate images referencing real-world events or entities from the current week by enabling web search grounding for factual accuracy.

prompt_templates.title: Quick draft generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a draft image with Nano Banana 2: a minimal product shot of [product] on a clean white background, studio lighting, e-commerce ready
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner — fast 1K square output for initial concepts

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic portrait with typography
prompt_templates.prompt: A cinematic close-up portrait of [subject description], shallow depth of field, reflective wet streets, ultra-detailed, realistic skin texture. The label reads 'BRAND' in clean bold sans-serif, centered.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate — combines subject-first grammar with quoted in-image text

prompt_templates.title: Vertical social hero with batch ideation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 vertical 9:16 hero variants for [brand/topic]: a single [object] on [surface], soft morning side-light, the words 'TAGLINE' in hand-drawn serif large at the top, gentle [detail], neutral color palette
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — batch mode with aspect ratio control and typography anchoring

prompt_templates.title: Web-grounded event illustration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a web-grounded image: a stylized illustration of [current event or real entity from this week], editorial style, suitable for a news article header, with enable_web_search enabled
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert — uses web grounding for factual accuracy on time-sensitive content

output_examples.input: User asks: 'Make me a cinematic portrait for my blog header'
output: Recognizes the request as a Nano Banana 2 use case (cinematic, in-image typography potential)
Generates at 16:9 aspect ratio, 2K resolution for blog header quality
Returns the runcomfy CLI command with a subject-first prompt and seed lock
Downloads the output PNG to the specified output directory

output_examples.input: User asks: 'I need 4 thumbnail ideas for a wellness brand Instagram post'
output: Identifies batch ideation use case — selects num_images: 4 and 0.5K resolution for fast iteration
Uses 9:16 aspect ratio for Instagram Stories / Reels native format
Generates 4 variants with consistent seed base for composition stability
Notes that the user can promote the best result to 2K in a follow-up call

output_examples.input: User asks: 'Create a poster with the text COFFEE in bold'
output: Applies the exact text quoting pattern: 'The label reads COFFEE in clean bold sans-serif'
Selects appropriate aspect ratio for poster format
Generates with predictable in-image typography rendering
Warns user that non-quoted text produces unpredictable results

best_practices: Use subject-first declarative grammar — front-load the primary subject, trail with style and camera directives
Quote exact characters for in-image typography — do not describe text abstractly, specify literal characters and font style
Lock the seed parameter when iterating a single prompt across small variants to keep composition stable
anti_patterns: Stacking conflicting styles like 'minimalist + ornate + retro + cyberpunk' — pick 1–2 anchors maximum
Verbally describing stable subject identity — use the edit endpoint with image references instead
Requesting resolutions outside the four supported tiers (0.5K, 1K, 2K, 4K) — will return a 422 error

faq.question: What is Nano Banana 2 and how does it differ from Nano Banana Pro?
faq.answer: Nano Banana 2 is the flash-tier text-to-image model in Google's Gemini family, optimized for rapid iteration and predictable framing. Nano Banana Pro is the pro tier designed for hyperrealistic portraits and maximum detail. Use 2 for drafts, social content, and typography; use Pro for portraits and final hero shots.

faq.question: How do I install the RunComfy CLI required by this skill?
faq.answer: Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli to install the CLI globally. Then run runcomfy login to authenticate via a browser device-code flow, or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments.

faq.question: What resolutions and aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Four resolution tiers: 0.5K (drafts), 1K (default), 2K (final), and 4K (maximum). Eleven aspect ratios including auto, 21:9, 16:9, 3:2, 4:3, 5:4, 1:1, 4:5, 3:4, 2:3, and 9:16. Requests outside these values will return a 422 error.

faq.question: How do I get predictable text rendering inside generated images?
faq.answer: Quote the exact characters you want rendered. Instead of 'with the brand name on it', write 'The label reads AURA in clean bold sans-serif, centered, white on black'. Specify placement and font style. Non-quoted text produces unpredictable results.

faq.question: Is there a batch mode for generating multiple variants?
faq.answer: Yes. Set num_images to 2, 3, or 4 in the input JSON. The model supports up to 4 images per request. Use a consistent seed base across rounds to keep composition stable while varying other parameters.

faq.question: When should I use the enable_web_search option?
faq.answer: Enable web search only when the prompt references current events, real entities, or time-sensitive factual content. Web grounding adds latency and cost, so it is off by default. For purely creative or generic prompts, leave it disabled.
